name: overpass_turbo
title: Query Geographic Data with Overpass Turbo
description: Find hospitals, EV charging stations, and schools in any city using plain English queries, then export as GeoJSON.
prompt: Go to overpass-turbo.eu, find all hospitals in Hyderabad, view on map, query EV charging stations, export as GeoJSON
narration_script_text:
Welcome to the cockpit, data hunters. Overpass Turbo is live. Calm canvas now, storm next.

Map nodes take a second. More math than high school. Boom, rendered.

Split view. Query wand on the left, map battlefield on the right.

Hospitals in Hyderabad. Fixed city box, full Overpass QL, nodes, ways, relations. Feed the machine.

Run it. The API is sprinting across the planet to fetch every hospital marker it can find.

The API is hauling hospital markers worldwide. Fast fiber beats virtue.

Blue circles everywhere. Zoom to data and teleport onto Hyderabad.

Click a marker and read the tags. Name, address, amenity type. Pure urban DNA.

Hit the Data tab. Raw OSM attributes. Nerd heaven.

Back to Map view so we can compare actual coverage.

Pivot time. Swap hospitals for EV chargers. Same city, totally different future.

Processing again. Come on, little data packets, do your thing.

Rendering complete. See those gaps? That is urban planning screaming for attention.

That spread tells you exactly where the future still needs building.

Now add schools. Education layer, same boundary. Watch the city grow in fast-forward.

Schools are loading. Keep the processors humming. City timelapse, live.

One more. Add pharmacies and build a four-layer dataset cake. City hall wishes it had this.

Final crunch. If the fan is spinning, that is just the sound of progress.

Single editor workflow. That is the superpower: iterate faster, analyze deeper, conquer the urban jungle.

One last pass. Look at that density. Pure data art.

Open Export. We are done playing; now we go pro.

GeoJSON, KML, GPX, raw OSM. Pick your ammo and ship the data.

Tutorial complete. Blank screen to geo-intelligence. Go bend maps to your will.
